items.title: Third Ukrainian strike hits Russian oil refinery and prompts evacuations
items.raw_content: Residents near a burning refinery in Tuapse are told to leave, and the Kremlin accuses Kyiv of destabilising global energy markets.

items.title: United Arab Emirates to quit oil cartel Opec
items.raw_content: The UAE leaving Opec is seen as a major blow and potential death knell for the oil cartel.

items.title: Sinner beats Norrie to equal historic winning run
items.raw_content: World number one Jannik Sinner's brilliant form continues as he reaches the quarter-finals of the Madrid Open with a straight-set win over Britain's Cameron Norrie.
items.ai_summary: 世界排名第一的辛纳直落两盘击败英国选手诺里，晋级马德里公开赛八强，延续连胜纪录。

items.title: 'I lived all my dreams' - Stones to leave Man City
items.raw_content: England defender John Stones says he "lived all my dreams out" at Manchester City after confirming he will leave at the end of the season.
items.ai_summary: 英格兰后卫斯通斯确认赛季末离开曼城，称已实现所有梦想。

items.title: I made 'serious mistake' advising Starmer to appoint Mandelson, PM's ex-top adviser McSweeney says
items.raw_content: Morgan McSweeney says the peer did not give the "full truth" about his relationship with Epstein.

items.title: Why Sam Altman and his former hero Elon Musk are taking their toxic feud to court

items.title: Three key statements Starmer made to MPs about Mandelson vetting

items.title: Man City left frustrated by fixture pile-up

items.title: Brighton plan Europe's first purpose-built £80m women's football stadium
